assets: ability to interpret public procurement records
strong judgment on recurring municipal buying failures
distribution: potential access to vendor-side and civic analysis communities
network_access: non-city buyer segments such as vendors, insurers, journalists, and watchdogs
constraints: must not use employer-confidential templates, pricing logic, or workflow details
credibility_signals: real operating exposure inside the govtech procurement category
informed understanding of where mid-sized cities waste money
unfair_advantages: can spot procurement waste patterns in public data faster than generalists
can design useful scoring frameworks for municipal procurement behavior
understands buyer pain without needing to learn the category from scratch

opportunities.title: Public procurement waste benchmark for adjacent buyers
opportunities.thesis: Start with a narrowly scoped benchmark built from public records, sell it as a recurring information product, and expand into a dataset or lightweight software.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user has superior judgment about municipal procurement waste patterns and can interpret public artifacts faster than a general operator without relying on employer IP.
validation_plan: Review employment and confidentiality restrictions and define written red lines.
Select one narrow spend category or friction theme for 25 cities.
Assemble a 10-city sample from public records only.
Publish a 2-page sample benchmark and test prepaid demand with adjacent buyers.
tool_specs: Public-record extraction workflow with LLM summarization
Static landing page with preorder flow
kill_criteria: Legal or policy conflict with employer restrictions
No ability to keep data and methodology fully independent of employer IP
No prepaid demand or qualified recurring interest after initial outreach

opportunities.title: Independent procurement modernization template library
opportunities.thesis: Create an original template and playbook library for city procurement teams, but only if conflict review shows the market and materials are safely outside employer boundaries.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user knows which procurement artifacts are operationally useful and could create better original materials than a non-expert.
validation_plan: Confirm no conflict with employer market, IP, or customer restrictions.
Draft one original template pack from scratch without referencing internal materials.
Test interest with non-customer municipal communities or associations.
kill_criteria: Any ambiguity around IP ownership or overlap with employer offerings
Buyers only want custom help instead of a self-serve library

opportunities.title: Productized public-record teardown reports
opportunities.thesis: Sell fixed-scope teardown reports on one city's procurement inefficiencies to adjacent buyers as a research product rather than consulting.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can diagnose public procurement problems quickly and package insight into a standardized report.
validation_plan: Create one standardized sample teardown from public documents.
Offer a fixed-price report to adjacent buyers only.
Measure whether demand is for the standardized product or for custom consulting.

opportunities.title: Vendor-side municipal bid qualification dataset
opportunities.thesis: Build a subscription dataset that helps SMB vendors decide whether a municipal RFP is worth pursuing based on public process-friction signals.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user understands which procurement features predict painful sales cycles and poor bid economics.
validation_plan: Interview 5 SMB vendors selling to municipalities.
Build a sample list of 20 RFPs with friction scores.
Test whether vendors will pay for qualification guidance before building software.
kill_criteria: Vendors do not see enough value over manual qualification
Data collection burden is too high for a solo operator at 5 hours per week

next_actions: Review employment restrictions and define written legal red lines before any market test.
Choose one narrow benchmark wedge and derive all metrics from public sources only.
Build a 10-city sample dataset and a 2-page benchmark preview.
Test prepaid demand with adjacent buyers who are not employer customers.
Continue only if demand appears for a recurring product rather than consulting.
